title: Mumbai ITAT Deletes ₹1.36 Crore Addition u/s 69A – Customs Duty Collections by CHA Are Fiduciary Receipts, Not Taxable Income
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that customs duty collections received by a CHA in a fiduciary capacity are not taxable under Section 69A and deleted the ₹1.36 crore addition. The post Mumbai ITAT Deletes ₹1.36 Crore Addition u/s 69A – Customs Duty Collections by CHA Are Fiduciary Receipts, Not Taxable Income appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted an addition of ₹1.36 Crore under Section 69A, ruling that customs duty collections by a Customs House Agent are fiduciary receipts and not taxable income. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of certain receipts in a fiduciary capacity.

title: SC Judgment in McDowell Redefined Tax Avoidance in India
summary: The Supreme Court held excise duty paid by buyers formed part of turnover, discussed colourable devices, and distinguished legitimate tax planning. The post SC Judgment in McDowell Redefined Tax Avoidance in India appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Supreme Court's McDowell judgment fundamentally redefined tax avoidance principles in India, distinguishing between legitimate tax planning and 'colourable devices.' This landmark ruling has broad implications for tax structuring and compliance across all tax domains, indirectly influencing transfer pricing strategies.

title: Section 43CA Inapplicable to AY 2013-14; Reopening Quashed: Bombay HC
summary: Bombay HC quashed Section 148 reopening for AY 2013-14, holding Section 43CA was inapplicable and stamp duty valuation alone could not justify addition. The post Section 43CA Inapplicable to AY 2013-14; Reopening Quashed: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court quashed a Section 148 reopening for AY 2013-14, ruling that Section 43CA was inapplicable for that assessment year. The court held that stamp duty valuation alone could not justify an addition for tax purposes related to property transactions.

title: Section 43CA Inapplicable to Pre-2013 Allotment Agreements and 5% Value Difference: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held Section 43CA inapplicable to pre-01.04.2013 allotment agreements and deleted the addition, accepting the 5% valuation difference. The post Section 43CA Inapplicable to Pre-2013 Allotment Agreements and 5% Value Difference: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that Section 43CA is inapplicable to allotment agreements executed prior to April 1, 2013. The tribunal also accepted a 5% valuation difference in the transaction, leading to the deletion of the tax addition related to property transfers.

title: No-Risk Status & Working Capital Adjustment Allowed in TP Analysis: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi remanded comparable selection, directed working capital adjustment, allowed risk adjustment, and partly allowed the transfer pricing appeal. The post No-Risk Status & Working Capital Adjustment Allowed in TP Analysis: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) remanded a transfer pricing case, allowing for a working capital adjustment and risk adjustment in the comparable selection. This significant decision emphasizes the importance of a thorough functional and risk analysis in determining arm's length pricing for related party transactions.

title: Transfer Pricing Documentation Checklist for FY 2025-26: Key Compliance Requirements

summary: ITAT Delhi held Section 68 inapplicable where shares were disclosed in an earlier year and sale proceeds were already offered as income, avoiding double taxation. The post Section 68 Addition on Sale of Previously Disclosed Shares Deleted to Avoid Double Taxation: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that an addition under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act was inapplicable where shares were previously disclosed and sale proceeds offered as income. This decision by the Indian tax tribunal prevents double taxation on share sales.

title: Captive Service Provider Entitled to Risk Adjustment; Nil ALP for Proven Intra-Group Services Rejected: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi held that documentary evidence established receipt of intra-group administrative support services and that the 5% markup was at arm's length. The transfer pricing adjustment was accordingly deleted. The post Captive Service Provider Entitled to Risk Adjustment; Nil ALP for Proven Intra-Group Services Rejected: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that a captive service provider was entitled to a risk adjustment, rejecting the tax authority's attempt to apply a nil arm's length price for proven intra-group administrative support services. The tribunal accepted a 5% markup as arm's length, leading to the deletion of the transfer pricing adjustment.

title: DCF Share Valuation Accepted as AO Could Not Replace Prescribed Method with NAV
summary: ITAT held that an Assessing Officer cannot substitute the DCF method chosen under Rule 11UA with the NAV method without legal justification. The post DCF Share Valuation Accepted as AO Could Not Replace Prescribed Method with NAV appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot arbitrarily substitute the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method for share valuation chosen under Rule 11UA with the Net Asset Value (NAV) method. This decision by the Indian tax tribunal affirms the taxpayer's right to use prescribed valuation methodologies.

title: Functionally dissimilar entities, entrepreneurs, and product developers were invalid comparables for captive IT service providers
summary: Transfer pricing principles dictate that a captive, risk-mitigated service provider could not be benchmarked against full-fledged, risk-bearing entrepreneurs, companies owning substantial intellectual property, or those generating revenue through software products. The post Functionally dissimilar entities, entrepreneurs, and product developers were invalid comparables for captive IT service providers appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This TaxGuru article, likely summarizing a recent judicial pronouncement from India, asserts that functionally dissimilar entities, such as entrepreneurs or product developers, are invalid comparables for captive, risk-mitigated IT service providers. It reinforces the importance of precise functional and risk analysis in comparability benchmarking.

title: TP Addition Deleted as Management Support Services Proved Genuine
summary: The ITAT Pune held that detailed agreements, invoices and supporting records established that management support services were actually rendered by the Associated Enterprise. It ruled that the payment was at arm's length and deleted the entire transfer pricing adjustment. The post TP Addition Deleted as Management Support Services Proved Genuine appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Pune deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that management support services provided by an Associated Enterprise were genuine and at arm's length. The decision was based on detailed agreements, invoices, and supporting records proving the services were actually rendered.

title: ITAT Delhi Excludes TPO Comparables for Functional Dissimilarity
summary: ITAT excluded EDCIL, Just Dial, Info Edge and India Exposition Mart as transfer pricing comparables due to functional differences and restored one comparable for verification. The post ITAT Delhi Excludes TPO Comparables for Functional Dissimilarity appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi excluded several comparables, including EDCIL, Just Dial, Info Edge, and India Exposition Mart, from transfer pricing benchmarking due to functional dissimilarities. The tribunal also directed verification for one restored comparable, emphasizing the critical role of strict comparability analysis.

title: Royalty TP Adjustment deleted as TNMM Accepted as Most Appropriate Method
summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad held that royalty payments should continue to be benchmarked under TNMM by following earlier decisions in the assessees own case. It found no reason to replace TNMM with CUP where identical facts had already been decided. The Department's transfer pricing challenge was therefore dismissed. The post Royalty TP Adjustment deleted as TNMM Accepted as Most Appropriate Method appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Ahmedabad deleted a transfer pricing adjustment on royalty payments, affirming the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) as the Most Appropriate Method. The tribunal rejected the tax department's attempt to apply CUP, citing consistency with previous decisions on identical facts for the assessee.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 56(2)(viib) Addition as AO Changed Valuation Method Contrary to Rule 11UA
summary: ITAT held that the AO could not change the valuation method adopted under Rule 11UA and deleted the addition under Section 56(2)(viib). The post ITAT Deletes Section 56(2)(viib) Addition as AO Changed Valuation Method Contrary to Rule 11UA appeared first on TaxGuru .
